manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: cedar, deacon, polyester, swan, gymnastics, france, birch, nitrogen, hat.
Your opponent's team words are: hockey, mercury, peru, wool, swallow, christmas, bishop, nuclear.
Distractor words are: ostrich, volleyball, korea, pine, pants, archbishop, acid.
The kill words are: coal.

message.reasoning:
The task: we need to provide a single-word clue and list of target words (our team's words) that the clue relates to. Must be not any of the listed words or contain parts of them. Must be semantically related.

Our team words: cedar, deacon, polyester, swan, gymnastics, france, birch, nitrogen... hat.

We can choose any subset, possibly many to be efficient. Need to avoid inadvertently linking to opponent or distractor words, because if partner guesses them, turn ends. We want clue that is strongly associated with our words, not opponent.

Opponent words: hockey, mercury, peru, wool, swallow, christmas, bishop, nuclear.

Distractor: ostrich, volleyball, korea, pine, pants, archbishop, acid.

Kill: coal.

Clue must not be any of these words nor contain parts of them. For example cannot be "bird" because swallow is opponent word; but swallow is opponent. "Bird" might also relate to swan, but also swallow -> opponent, risk. So avoid.

We want a clue that points to, say, "swan" and maybe "hat"? Could be "ballet"? That relates to gymnastics, swan (ballet swan lake), hat? maybe not. Also "France" could relate to "Paris". But French... Not in list.

We could aim for a clue that relates to cedar, birch, polyester (synthetic fiber), nitrogen (element), hat (clothing), deacon (religious), gymnastics (sport), swan (bird), france (country). Hard to cover many.

Maybe clue "nature" relates to cedar, birch, swan, nitrogen. But also could relate to "wool" (opponent). But wool is a distractor? Actually wool is opponent's team word. So nature maybe implies wool? Not strongly. Could be ambiguous.

Clue "bird" would point to swan but also swallow (opponent) and ostrich (distractor). So not good.

Clue "fabric" relates to polyester, maybe hat? but not strong.

Clue "church" relates to deacon (team) and bishop (opponent) and archbishop (distractor). So risky.

Clue "sports" relates to gymnastics (team) and hockey (opponent) and volleyball (distractor). Risky.

Clue "tree" relates to cedar, birch, pine (distractor). Might cause picking pine. So risk.

Clue "element" relates to nitrogen (team) and mercury (opponent) and acid (distractor). Risk.

Clue "French" relates to france (team) and maybe "peru"? Not. Could also be "bishop"? Not.

Clue "headwear" relates to hat (team) maybe also "cap"? Not in list. Not opponent.

Clue "water" might link to swan, nitrogen (dissolved), but also mercury (opponent). Risk.

Better idea: choose a clue that only strongly points to a subset of our words with minimal risk. Could choose clue "synth" for polyester, maybe nitrogen (synthetic?), not.

Maybe clue "Lake" relates to swan (swan lake) and maybe "France" (Lake?). Not opponent. But could also link to "Mercury" (Lake Mercury?) Not.

Clue "Feathers" indicates swan (bird) but also could lead to ostrich (distractor) and swallow (opponent). So risk.

Maybe aim for three words: "gymnastics" for gymnastics and "gym"? Not.

Maybe clue "sport" too ambiguous.

What about "clothing"? relates to hat, polyester, maybe birch? Not.

Clue "winter" could hint at hat, swan (cold), but also christmas (opponent). Risk.

Perhaps choose two target words with a unique clue. Eg clue "Cedar" cannot.

Clue "birdwatching" likewise.

Better to target "deacon, france, hat" with clue "clergy"? That relates to deacon (team) and bishop (opponent), archbishop (distractor). Not good.

Maybe choose "swan" alone with clue "grace". Swans are graceful; gymnastics also graceful; maybe also "hat" (graceful hat?). Not.

But guess list can be multiple. The fewer words we risk, maybe easier.

We must output CLUE and TARGETS list.

Probably safest: pick a clue that only strongly matches one team word, reducing chance of opponent guess. For example clue "polymer" for polyester. Does not match opponent words. Might also hint at nitrogen? Not. So we can just give one target.

Allowed? Yes, we can choose any number.

Thus clue "polymer" TARGETS: polyester. Ensure clue is not part of any words; polymer is not same but includes "poly". "poly" is part of polyester, but that's okay? The clue cannot contain parts of the words? It says cannot contain parts of them. "polymer" contains "poly". That's a part of polyester (prefix poly). Might be considered part. Safer choose "synthetic". That is related to polyester. Does any opponent include "synthetic"? No. Also not part of any listed words. Good.

So CLUE: Synthetic, TARGETS: polyester.

Check risk: synthetic also could hint at "wool"? Actually wool is natural fiber, not synthetic. So not.

Thus output
